description: Maintainability: BillingGuard implements a clear 4-layer resolution logic (Admin Override -> Manifest -> Module Default -> Default Policy) which is easy to reason about and debug.

Pattern Consistency: BillingGuard strictly follows the project's 'preload-at-startup, sync-read-at-runtime' pattern established in infra-policy-guard.ts, ensuring zero I/O overhead during tRPC request processing.
Integration: Global billing middleware is correctly scoped to 'pluginApis' routes, preventing impact on core procedures and aligning with the plugin system's boundaries.
Initialization: Startup order in TrpcModule correctly waits for PluginModule (via imports) to ensure manifests are loaded before BillingGuard attempts to resolve L3 capabilities.
Observability: The 'audit' policy for undeclared billing provides excellent visibility (console warning + audit_logs record) without breaking the user experience during configuration phases.

Pattern Divergence: The audit log for undeclared billing in trpc.ts uses direct db.insert(auditLogs) instead of the buffered scheduleAuditFlush pattern. While consistent with other security logs (Auth/RBAC), it bypasses the batch-write logic of the main audit system.
